Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease with nephrotic features. Low specific gravity may indicate impaired concentrating ability. Hemoglobin A1c is low, making diabetes unlikely despite glucosuria. Electrolytes are mostly within range, but chloride is slightly elevated. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal pathology.
